§6Reclaimed Industrial Oil§f

§aRecycled industrial oil — the base fluid for petroil production and an efficient solid fuel source!§f

Reclaimed Industrial Oil (also called recycled oil) is produced by processing §bIndustrial Oil§f in the §bChemical Plant§f. It has low fuel value (§e113 kTU§f / §e141 kHE§f per bucket) and is primarily used as the base fluid for §bPetroil§f production.

§6Production§f:
§b• Chemical Plant§f: §e1,000mB§f Industrial Oil → §e800mB§f Reclaimed Industrial Oil (requires §bReclaimed Oil§f template).

§6Uses§f:
§b• Petroil§f (Industrial Mixer): §e800mB§f + §e200mB§f Engine Lubricant → §e1,000mB§f Petroil (the primary use).
§b• Solid Fuel§f: §e15,000mB§f → §e1×§f (Industrial Solidification) or §e6,300mB§f → §e1×§f (Pyrolysis Oven).
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e5,800mB§f → §e1×§f Petroleum Coke + §e580mB§f Coker Naphtha.

§eTip§f: The §e20%§f volume loss from Industrial Oil to Reclaimed Industrial Oil is worth it — the resulting Petroil has better fuel properties and enables further processing. If you have excess Industrial Oil, this is an excellent way to utilize it!
